mechanics.excerpt: INTRODUCTION sents the setting and situation; through their characters the players interact with the story line and other characters_ Think of it this way: everyone has read book or seen movie in which the lead character does something that the read: er Or viewer finds so utterly wrong that he or she wants t0 shout out warning: But no matter what we say; the character will do what the plot demands; we're just along the ride_ Even throwing popcorn won' nelp: roleplaying game_ the players control their characters' actions and respond to the events the plot: If the player does not want his Or her character t0 go through the door; the char acter will not: If the player thinks the character can talk him- or herself out of tight situation rather than resorting to that trusty pistol, he 0r she can talk away: The script, or plot, of roleplaying game is flexible always changing based on the deci- sions the players make as characters The gamemaster controls the story: He or she keeps track of what is supposed happen when _ describes events as they occur SO that the players (as characters) can react t0 them_ keeps track other characters in the game (referred t0 as non- player characters , or NPCs), and resolves attempts t0 take action using the game system.
